market_summary: Major risk-on/risk-off whipsaw session. Early rally on bogus report that Iran-US resolution was 'near' sent Dow +645pts (+1.3%) to 50,009, Nasdaq +1.6% to 26,270, Russell 2000 higher, oil briefly below $100. Rally faded as Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei issued directive ordering uranium to remain on Iranian soil, contradicting deal conditions — gold fell toward $4,500, oil rebounded to ~$99.5 WTI / $108.76 Brent. FOMC minutes released 2pm ET from April meeting (Powell's last as chair) showed hawkish split: majority see rate hike warranted if inflation persists, concerns about inflation expectations de-anchoring, elevated energy/tariffs embedding broadly. DXY rose to 99.39 (+0.30%). Nvidia reported after close: revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY), EPS $1.87 beating $1.75, Q2 guide $91B vs $86.8B consensus — stock opened down 0.5% on Thursday as 'garden variety beat' failed to excite. Goldman Sachs +5.8% (best Dow gainer). Intuit -15% on 17% workforce reduction. SoftBank surged 19.85% in Tokyo on Nvidia results. BTC drifted to $77.3K (-2.5% weekly), ETH $2,139. Gold $4,517-4,538, silver $75.30.
key_events_tomorrow: Conference Board Leading Economic Index (LEI) release at 10:00 AM ET — forward-looking recession indicator
University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment (Final May) at 10:00 AM ET — consensus 55.5, inflation expectations key
State Employment and Unemployment data (April) at 10:00 AM ET
62 total economic events scheduled per Yahoo Finance — heavy Friday data day
Nvidia post-earnings trading continues — $91B Q2 guide reaction across semis/AI names
Iran-US deal headlines remain binary catalyst — Khamenei uranium directive contradicts deal framework
Kevin Warsh Fed transition in focus — first FOMC June 16-17, markets pricing near-zero chance of 2026 rate cut
overnight_risks: Iran deal collapse risk elevated — Khamenei directive on uranium staying in Iran directly contradicts Israeli/US deal conditions, peace talks on 'life support' per Trump
Oil gap risk: WTI at $99.5, Brent $108.76 — Strait of Hormuz still largely blocked since Feb 28, any escalation headline sends Brent above $115
Nvidia post-earnings drift: despite beat+raise, stock opened -0.5% — if selling accelerates, tech/AI complex reprices Friday
FOMC minutes hawkish tilt: majority policymakers see rate hike as likely warranted — bond market may reprice rates higher, pressuring risk assets
Asia strong: Nikkei +3.14% to 61,684 (SoftBank +20%, semis rallied hard) — divergence from US tepid reaction to Nvidia creates reversion risk
DXY at 99.39 and rising on hawkish Fed + oil inflation — continued dollar strength pressures commodities and crypto
Silver fragile at $75.30 — below $73 support from May 19 opens path to $70 if risk-off resumes
Samsung strike averted (47K workers, South Korea) — if negotiations break down again, semiconductor supply chain risk resurfaces

instrument_news.BTC-USD: Bitcoin at $77,262-$77,852, down 2.51% on the week. Opened week at $79,850, hit brief high near $81,500 on May 15 then sold off to $76,000 low on May 19 before partial recovery. Market cap $1.33T. Iran peace talk optimism providing marginal tailwind ('final stages' per Trump) but conviction behind bearish bias lowest all month. FOMC minutes showing hawkish split is negative for risk assets including crypto. No BTC-specific catalyst. ETF inflows recovering but macro headwinds (Fed hike risk, $100+ oil, DXY at 99.4) dominate. Support $76K (May 19 low), resistance $80K.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: Ethereum at $2,116-$2,139, +1.25% in 24h, market cap $258B. Range-bound $2,100-$2,200 with weak momentum. ETF outflows and macro liquidity tightening weighing. Rising oil prices add external risk via inflation transmission. Vitalik Buterin ETH sales earlier in 2026 still an overhang on sentiment. Down from near $5,000 ATH (Aug 2025). No ETH-specific catalyst. MEXC analysis: downward pressure expected to continue, investors should remain alert to sell-offs triggered by rising oil. Support $2,100, resistance $2,200.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold fell 0.47% to $4,517 (TradingEconomics) / $4,538 (Investing.com). Down 4.70% on the month but +36.89% YoY. Fell toward $4,500 as Iran peace hopes faded after Khamenei uranium directive. FOMC minutes hawkish tilt (rate hike warranted) is negative for gold via higher real yields. DXY at 99.39 rising is headwind. However central bank buying remains robust: Q1 2026 record 337t, full-year projection 700-900t. Gold ETFs saw $0.83B inflows in April (still -$1.5B YTD after record $13B March outflow). Support $4,450-$4,500, resistance $4,700.
instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver at $75.305, recovering from earlier weakness. Rose 2.71% on May 20 to $75.67. FXEmpire: bulls targeting break above $76.75 confluence hurdle. Sixth consecutive supply deficit (46M oz) remains structural support. Industrial demand from solar/EV provides floor. More volatile than gold on geopolitical swings — vulnerable to risk-off with Iran headline risk. FOMC hawkish minutes add headwind via real yield channel. Support $73 (May 19 low), resistance $76.75 then $84-87.
instrument_news.MSTR: Strategy Inc (formerly MicroStrategy) at $165.81, down 9.65% over past week. Market cap $57.69B. Filed proxy statement May 19 for shareholder voting. Holds massive BTC treasury. TD Cowen raised PT to $400 from $395 (Buy). Shares declined 6.8% amid falling Bitcoin prices and investor concerns about potential BTC sales. Analysts project 140% upside driven by aggressive BTC acquisition strategy. Next earnings Aug 4, 2026. Directly correlated to BTC — range-bound BTC means range-bound MSTR. BTC weakness this week ($77K vs $80K last week) dragging stock.

metals_outlook: Gold and silver caught in crosswinds. Bearish near-term: FOMC minutes hawkish (rate hike on table), DXY rising to 99.39, Iran peace deal fading but not resolved (geopolitical premium unstable). Gold at $4,517 down 4.7% monthly — first meaningful pullback since war began. Central bank buying remains the structural floor: Q1 record 337t, full-year 700-900t expected. Gold ETFs had $0.83B April inflows partially recovering the $13B record March outflow. COT: large specs near -9.2K, approaching net-long flip — not stretched. Silver at $75.30 with sixth consecutive supply deficit, but more volatile on macro swings. India 15% duty hike remains headwind for physical demand. Key risk: if Warsh signals actual rate hike at June FOMC (16-17), gold could break $4,450 support. Key catalyst: Iran deal collapse would send gold above $4,700. Net: range-bound $4,450-$4,700 gold, $73-$77 silver until Iran/Fed clarity emerges.
oil_outlook: Oil remains the dominant macro variable. WTI at $99.5 (fell ~6% over two prior sessions on Iran deal hopes), Brent at $108.76. Strait of Hormuz largely blocked since Feb 28 Iran war — shipping at ~5% of pre-conflict volume. WTI/Brent up 45%+ since war began. Today's whipsaw: bogus Iran deal report briefly sent WTI below $100, then Khamenei uranium directive crushed optimism. Trump rejected Iran counteroffer as 'garbage', ceasefire on 'life support'. OPEC+ modest 188K bpd increase in early May (less than prior 206K). EIA forecasts $106/b for May-June. Global inventories falling 8.5M b/d Q2. Iran military capacity restoring faster than expected, stoking renewed conflict fears. Binary outcome: genuine deal = $10-15 crash in oil; deal collapse = Brent $115-120+. Oil above $100 is structurally inflationary and directly pressures Fed toward hike, cascading negatively to all risk assets.
crypto_outlook: BTC at $77.3K and ETH at $2.1K both range-bound and macro-driven. Hawkish FOMC minutes (rate hike on table) negative for crypto via rate differential and risk appetite. DXY at 99.39 rising is headwind. Oil above $100 drives inflation which keeps Fed hawkish which hurts crypto — transmission chain intact. Nvidia beat+raise mildly positive for risk sentiment but market reaction tepid. Iran deal binary: genuine peace = oil crash = inflation relief = Fed easing expectations = crypto positive. ETH-specific: weak ETF flows, $2,100-$2,200 range, macro liquidity tightening dominates. BTC support $76K, resistance $80K. ETH support $2,100, resistance $2,200. No breakout catalyst visible until Iran/Fed picture clarifies.
sentiment_shift: Sentiment whipsawed from cautious optimism (Iran deal hopes, Nvidia beat) to renewed anxiety (Khamenei uranium directive, hawkish FOMC minutes). Net shift: marginally more hawkish/risk-off than yesterday. Key developments shifting sentiment: (1) FOMC minutes revealed majority of policymakers see rate hike as warranted — market had been pricing near-zero hike probability, this reprices, (2) Kevin Warsh confirmed May 13 but faces 'family fight' on rates with inflation at 6% wholesale and oil above $100, (3) Iran peace deal credibility damaged by Khamenei directive — binary geopolitical risk persists. The Nvidia 'garden variety beat' failed to spark risk-on despite +85% revenue growth. Market is Fed-and-Iran driven, not earnings-driven. Rate hike pricing increasing: <3% chance of any cut in 2026, growing camp sees September hike. Until oil drops below $90 or Iran deal materializes, sustained risk-on is unlikely.
fed_update: FOMC minutes from April 29 meeting (Powell's last as chair) released at 2pm ET May 21. Key takeaways: (1) Majority of officials see rate hike as likely warranted if inflation persists, (2) Officials judge rate pause will extend longer than previously thought, (3) Some concerned inflation expectations could de-anchor, (4) Some warned elevated energy prices and tariffs could embed inflation more broadly. Kevin Warsh confirmed as new Fed chair May 13 (54-45 vote), takes seat at June 16-17 FOMC. Despite expectations Warsh favors cuts, wholesale inflation at 6%, oil above $100, and shelter inflation doubling in April force consideration of hikes instead. Market pricing: near-zero chance of rate cut in 2026, growing probability of rate hike by September.
geopolitical: Iran-US conflict remains the dominant market driver. Strait of Hormuz blocked since Feb 28 when US/Israel launched air war against Iran. Key May 21 developments: (1) Bogus report of imminent resolution briefly spiked equities and crashed oil, (2) Iran's Khamenei issued directive ordering uranium to remain on Iranian soil — contradicts core Israeli/US deal requirement, (3) Iran restoring military capacity faster than expected, (4) Trump called Iran counteroffer 'garbage', ceasefire 'on life support'. Binary outcomes: deal = oil -$15, gold -$200, equities +5%, crypto +10%. Collapse = oil +$15, gold +$200, equities -5%, crypto -5%. Samsung South Korea strike averted (47K workers) — positive for semiconductor supply chain.
